query: I want 95% prediction intervals (alpha = 0.05) around a regression model that predicts patient hospital length-of-stay. The full dataset is small — I can carve off at most 150 rows as a calibration set. Is split conformal still viable here, and what should I worry about?
expected_behavior: Recognizes that a 150-point calibration set at alpha = 0.05 leaves only a handful of points in the tail above the conformal quantile, making the quantile estimate noisy and the resulting interval width unstable across different calibration draws

Offers at least one concrete mitigation — for example dropping the coverage target to 90% so the quantile sits on more tail points, switching to jackknife+ or cross-conformal that reuses data more efficiently, or collecting more calibration data — instead of silently producing a 95% interval at the requested sample size
Does NOT claim that split conformal at n_cal = 150 with alpha = 0.05 produces a stable 95% interval without naming the variance / imprecision issue
